purpose: Operational contract for AI-generated screen JSON in the UIBuilder SwiftUI runtime. Optimize for valid structure, correct semantic node choice, and minimal primitive overcomposition.

authoringMindset.primaryRule: Choose the highest-level semantic node that matches the UI intent. Use primitive layout nodes only when no semantic node expresses the intent directly.
nonAuthoritativeInputs: Demo and example JSON files in the repository illustrate renderer features for humans, but they are not canonical AI instructions.
The capabilities file is not the AI contract. It mixes runtime inventory, examples, and authoring advice.
semanticPreference: Treat list, section, navigationstack, toolbaritem, group, menu, tabview, tab, label, image, textfield, slider, shape, and table as semantic authoring tools, not decorative aliases.
Treat vstack, hstack, zstack, spacer, scroll, and frame as structural helpers. They are not the default worldview for every screen.
Prefer authoring meaning first, then layout. Start from the screen role, not from stack geometry.

decisionOrder: Pick the strongest semantic root container first: navigationstack, tabview, list, or a freeform root only when semantics do not call for a stronger container.

Use semantic structure next: section for grouped rows, group for functional subsections, toolbaritem for chrome actions, table for column-driven records, label for icon+text units, textfield/slider/toggle for interactive controls.
Apply primitive layout only for local arrangement that semantic nodes do not already express.
Author only the explicit structure, props, bindings, and event names; leave standard container-owned presentation to the runtime.
Validate actions, expressions, props, and enum values against the canonical syntax and reference surface before finalizing.

notes: root must be a single UINode tree.
events is the top-level map of named action arrays referenced by node.event values.
event values are event names, not inline action arrays.
items exists in the schema but is not a primary authoring mechanism for the current supported semantic nodes. Prefer explicit child nodes.

authorMustExpress: The semantic structure of the screen.
All explicit children and local composition.
Required props and supported enum values.
Conditional visibility and enabled rules through visibleWhen and enabledWhen.
Section boundaries, toolbaritem placement, tab membership, and table/list grouping.
rendererOwns: Standard scroll content flow; authors should not add an extra wrapper node unless local layout demands it.
Toolbar structure and title presentation for navigationstack.
Tab item presentation and standard tab content flow.
Group presentation choices driven by group props such as style and collapsible.
Table header presentation described by columns/headerVisible rather than hand-authored header rows.
Shared visual styling from supported props such as padding, size, colors, borders, shadows, clipping, and opacity.
doNotAuthor: Do not add extra wrapper nodes for standard scroll or tab content flow.
Unsupported node aliases or made-up props.
Alternative expression syntaxes not supported by the runtime decoder.

semanticDecisionRules.question: Should the screen own navigation chrome or toolbar actions?
when: The screen needs a navigation title.
The screen has top-bar or bottom-bar actions.
The screen should read as a navigable destination rather than a plain content block.
semanticDecisionRules.because: navigationstack carries navigation semantics and toolbar structure that stacks do not.

semanticDecisionRules.question: Are you presenting row-oriented settings, menu options, records, or document-style vertical content?
when: Items should behave like rows rather than freeform stacked blocks.
The screen resembles settings, inbox, grouped options, or a vertical collection of entries.
Section grouping is meaningful.
semanticDecisionRules.because: list expresses row semantics; a plain vstack only expresses vertical placement.

semanticDecisionRules.question: Do rows belong to labeled groups inside a list or table?
when: Rows need a semantic header.
Related rows should be grouped as one labeled unit.
You want list/table grouping rather than a decorative titled card.
semanticDecisionRules.because: section communicates grouped row structure to the renderer.

semanticDecisionRules.question: Is a toolbar control part of the screen chrome rather than ordinary content?
when: The control belongs in a navigation bar or bottom bar.
Its placement matters as leading, trailing, or bottom chrome.
insteadOf: placing the same button as the first content row
semanticDecisionRules.because: toolbaritem is structurally meaningful only as a direct child of navigationstack.

semanticDecisionRules.question: Is the content a titled or collapsible semantic subsection rather than a generic stack?
when: Children form one functional unit with a title and optional footer.
The content should read as a card, inset section, or accordion/disclosure block.
The grouping meaning should survive even if layout changes later.
insteadOf: vstack with manual title text and box styling
semanticDecisionRules.because: group carries semantic section intent and optional disclosure behavior.

semanticDecisionRules.question: Is the UI tab-based at the screen root or major shell level?
when: There are multiple peer destinations switched by tabs.
Each destination has its own content subtree.
insteadOf: manual button-driven tab bars built from stacks
semanticDecisionRules.because: tabview and tab represent tab semantics explicitly.

semanticDecisionRules.question: Do you need a compound icon+text value as one semantic label?
when: The icon and text form one single piece of meaning.
You would otherwise build hstack + icon + text only for a label-like row.
insteadOf: hstack containing icon and text only
semanticDecisionRules.because: label is the canonical semantic node for icon+text.

semanticDecisionRules.question: Do you need a standalone image or visual asset?
when: The visual should scale as an image.
It is a content image or large symbol-like visual.
semanticDecisionRules.because: icon is for compact SF Symbol glyphs, while image is the semantic image node.

semanticDecisionRules.question: Do you need text input?
when: The user edits a single-line string value.
The input needs placeholder, secure entry, keyboard type, or prefix/suffix icons.
insteadOf: text plus shape plus button composition pretending to be an input
semanticDecisionRules.because: textfield owns binding and input behavior.

semanticDecisionRules.question: Do you need adjustable numeric input over a range?
when: The value is continuous or stepped.
The user should drag within min/max bounds.
insteadOf: buttons and text assembled to imitate a range control
semanticDecisionRules.because: slider expresses range adjustment semantics directly.

semanticDecisionRules.question: Do you need geometric decoration or a shape-backed visual container?
when: A rectangle, roundedRectangle, circle, capsule, or ellipse is the visual primitive.
Children should sit on top of a geometric background.
insteadOf: using generic stacks only for colored geometry
semanticDecisionRules.because: shape is the supported semantic geometry node.

semanticDecisionRules.question: Is the content truly tabular with columns and row-like records?
when: Column headers matter.
Rows read as records in a small data table rather than settings rows.
insteadOf: list or vstack when columns are semantically important
semanticDecisionRules.because: table communicates tabular intent and owns optional header presentation.

semanticDecisionRules.question: When are primitive stacks still appropriate?
semanticDecisionRules.choose: vstack, hstack, zstack, spacer, frame, scroll
when: You need local layout not covered by a semantic node.
Children are heterogeneous freeform content rather than rows, tabs, toolbar items, or titled semantic groups.
The stack exists to solve layout, not to imitate a semantic container.
semanticDecisionRules.caution: Primitive stacks are fallback tools. Use them intentionally, not by default.

eventBinding.rule: node.event maps event keys such as tap or change to top-level event names.

notes: Only top-level events contain action arrays.
Use the exact action names supported by UIActionExecutor.
Keep forward-compatible fields such as event and payload only under top-level action objects, not under node.event.

expressions.runtimeStatus: Current runtime decoding uses key-based expression objects such as bind, eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte, and, or, and not, plus literal values.
repositoryUsageNotes: The canonical comparison shape is {"eq": [left, right]} and the sibling forms use the same array-of-operands pattern.
This key-based syntax is what Expression.swift decodes and what the main bundled screen files use for live authoring behavior.
Some showcase/example files may still contain legacy op/left/right-style examples; those files are not the protocol source of truth for current generation.

antiPatterns.name: stack-first screen design
antiPatterns.avoid: Starting every screen with vstack/hstack and reconstructing semantics manually.
antiPatterns.prefer: Select navigationstack, list, section, group, menu, tabview, table, label, textfield, slider, image, or shape when they match the intent.

antiPatterns.name: manual list from vstack rows
antiPatterns.avoid: Using a vstack of repeated rows for settings/document content that is semantically a list.

antiPatterns.name: manual section headers
antiPatterns.avoid: Using text + vstack to simulate grouped list sections.

antiPatterns.name: toolbar as content row
antiPatterns.avoid: Placing toolbar actions as the first visible row in screen content when they belong in chrome.
antiPatterns.prefer: Use navigationstack with direct toolbaritem children.

antiPatterns.name: fake group cards
antiPatterns.avoid: Using vstack + title text + background styling when the block is actually one semantic subsection.

antiPatterns.name: manual button label composition
antiPatterns.avoid: Giving button children or building hstack + icon + text only to create a button label.

antiPatterns.name: manual label composition
antiPatterns.avoid: Using hstack + icon + text when the unit is just an icon-text label.

antiPatterns.name: fake input controls
antiPatterns.avoid: Assembling text, shape, and buttons to imitate input fields or range controls.

antiPatterns.name: redundant scroll/tab wrappers
antiPatterns.avoid: Adding an outer vstack inside scroll or tab only because scrolling/tab content needs a container.
antiPatterns.prefer: Rely on the container's standard content flow unless extra nesting is truly needed for local layout.

antiPatterns.name: unsupported expression syntax
antiPatterns.avoid: Using op/left/right or string expression languages.
antiPatterns.prefer: Use the canonical expression object forms supported by the decoder.

antiPatterns.name: invented runtime API
antiPatterns.avoid: Generating unsupported nodes, unsupported action names, or made-up enum values.
antiPatterns.prefer: Stay inside the documented supported surface.

fallbackPolicy.rule: If no semantic node matches the intent, use the smallest primitive layout needed to realize the structure.
order: Add primitive layout only for local arrangement inside that semantic container.
Use frame only when explicit sizing/alignment is actually required.
Use zstack only for genuine layering.
Use scroll only when scrolling is required.
Do not replace an available semantic node with a larger stack-based imitation.

finalAuthoringChecklist: Use only supported nodes, actions, and enum values.
Choose semantic nodes before primitive stacks.
Keep all actions in the top-level events map.
Use list for row-oriented content and section for grouped rows.
Use navigationstack for navigable screens with titles or toolbar actions.
Use group for semantic subsections, not decorative wrapper stacks.
Let containers own their standard content flow and toolbar structure.
Add primitive layout only where it solves real local layout needs.

semanticFirstDefaultExamples.usageRule: These are the default generation patterns when the UI intent matches. Prefer them over freeform stack composition.

navigableSettingsScreen.why: Use list + section, not a large vstack of rows.

functionalSemanticGroup.why: Use group for a functional subsection with title/footer/collapsible behavior.

fallbackFreeformExamples.usageRule: Use these only when no stronger semantic container matches the intent. They are fallback patterns, not the default screen-building model.

freeformLayoutFallback.why: Primitive layout is appropriate when the content is genuinely freeform and no semantic container fits better.

nodeReference.referenceUseRule: This is the secondary reference section. Use it after the protocol core, decision rules, and anti-pattern guidance.

guidance: Choose navigationstack when navigation chrome or toolbar structure is part of the screen.
Choose tabview when the screen is primarily tab-based.
Choose list when the whole screen is fundamentally row-oriented.
Choose scroll or vstack only when the screen is freeform content without stronger semantics.

notes: Use local props before adding wrapper nodes whose only purpose is styling or simple layout.
maxWidth may use the string value infinity.
fixedSize may be authored as one of the documented string values, and the runtime also accepts boolean true for a fully fixed view.

navigationstack.purpose: Root navigation container with title and toolbar semantics.

childrenPolicy.otherChildren: Ordinary page content authored under the navigation container.

preferWhen: The screen needs navigation bar title or toolbar actions.
The screen is a destination-like page.
avoidWhen: The node is being used as a decorative shell rather than a navigation container.
The screen has no navigation semantics at all and no toolbar/title needs.
authorMustState: Toolbar items as direct children when needed.
The content subtree as non-toolbar children.
props.title if a navigation title is desired.
rendererDoes: Toolbar structure is owned by the navigation container.
Title presentation is owned by navigationstack.title.
Authors provide ordinary page content as non-toolbar children; standard screen flow does not need an extra helper wrapper.

toolbaritem.purpose: Semantic toolbar content for navigation chrome.
preferWhen: An action belongs in top-leading, top-trailing, or bottom toolbar space.
avoidWhen: The control is ordinary page content rather than chrome.
The node is not a direct child of navigationstack.
authorMustState: placement when not using the default trailing behavior.
children that define the visible toolbar control, typically button, menu, or label.
rendererDoes: Placement is interpreted by the navigation container when toolbaritem is a direct navigationstack child.
Outside navigationstack, toolbaritem does not create toolbar chrome and behaves like ordinary content.

preferWhen: The screen or region is primarily a list of rows.
Settings-like content or grouped options are being authored.
section nodes are meaningful.
avoidWhen: The content is a single freeform marketing/layout block.
The structure is truly tabular and table fits better.
authorMustState: Each row as a child node.
section children where grouping is semantically meaningful.
rendererDoes: List presentation is owned by the list container.
Authors should add local layout only when a row needs it, not to recreate list semantics.

section.purpose: Semantic row grouping inside list or table-like content.
preferWhen: Rows belong to a named subgroup.
A header should represent the group rather than exist as a separate content row.
avoidWhen: You only need visual spacing between arbitrary blocks.
The grouping is better represented as group because it is not row semantics.
authorMustState: Children that belong to the section.
props.title when the section needs a header.
rendererDoes: section.title becomes the section header without a separate header row node.
Authors provide only the grouped rows that belong to the section.

group.purpose: Semantic functional subsection with optional title, footer, style, and collapsible disclosure behavior.
preferWhen: Related controls or readouts form one functional unit.
A card-like or inset semantic subsection is desired.
An accordion/disclosure block is needed.
avoidWhen: You only need raw vertical layout with no semantic subsection meaning.
The content is truly list rows and section is the better fit.
authorMustState: Children that belong to the unit.
title and footer only when they are semantically meaningful.
collapsible and initial isExpanded when disclosure behavior is intended.
rendererDoes: Group presentation comes from the group props; authors do not need helper wrapper nodes for standard card, inset, or collapsible behavior.

table.purpose: Tabular records with optional column headers and row selection behavior.
preferWhen: Columns carry meaning.
Rows are record-like rather than settings-like.
avoidWhen: You just need a standard settings list.
Column structure is not semantically important.
authorMustState: Children as row views or section groups.
columns when a header row is needed.
selectionMode only from the supported enum values.
rendererDoes: Table header presentation comes from columns and headerVisible.
Authors provide the record rows or section groups, not a manually duplicated table shell.
supportNotes: sortable remains part of the declared authoring surface for forward compatibility and should not be invented under a different name.
selectionMode accepts none, single, or multiple; multiple is declared even though current behavior is limited.

tabview.purpose: Top-level or major multi-destination tab container.
preferWhen: Peer destinations are switched by tabs.
Each tab owns a content subtree.
avoidWhen: You are faking tabs with buttons and conditional stacks.
The screen only has one destination.
authorMustState: Direct tab children.
selection binding key only when programmatic tab switching is needed.
rendererDoes: Tab item presentation is derived from each tab node.
Tab content does not require an extra structural wrapper unless local layout demands it.

preferWhen: The node is a direct child of tabview and represents one tab.
avoidWhen: Used outside tabview.
Used only as a decorative nested grouping node.
authorMustState: title for the tab item.
icon when an SF Symbol tab icon is desired.
The tab content children.
rendererDoes: Tab item presentation comes from title and icon.
Tab content does not require an extra structural wrapper unless local layout demands it.

menu.purpose: Contextual action chooser with a trigger label and action children.
preferWhen: Multiple related actions should appear from one trigger.
The UI intent is a contextual menu, not a visible button row.
avoidWhen: There is only one action and a button is clearer.
The choices should be always visible rows instead of a contextual menu.
authorMustState: Action children, usually buttons.
Either a dedicated label child with id='label' or type='label', or fallback title/icon props for the trigger.
rendererDoes: Uses the special label child as the trigger when present.
Otherwise builds the trigger from title/icon props.

preferWhen: A tappable action is needed.
The control should be represented as one clear action element.
avoidWhen: You are about to author button children or hand-build a button label from separate child nodes.
The intent is really a row label or toolbar placement structure.
authorMustState: The label prop as props.label.
props.icon only when an SF Symbol should accompany the label.
event.tap when the button should perform an action.
rendererDoes: Builds the visible button label from the label prop (props.label) and optional props.icon.
button.childrenPolicy: Do not author children. Button content is defined by props.label and optional props.icon.

preferWhen: Icon and text together express one single item or row label.
avoidWhen: You need a tappable action; use button.
You need a scalable standalone image; use image.
authorMustState: title.
icon only when it improves meaning.
rendererDoes: Creates the Label view automatically.

preferWhen: A symbol or asset should behave like an image and scale visually.
avoidWhen: The visual is only a small inline glyph beside text; use icon or label.
authorMustState: Exactly one of systemName or name.
contentMode when fill behavior is needed.
rendererDoes: Creates a resizable image and applies aspect behavior.

preferWhen: A small standalone glyph is enough.
The glyph is not the main content image.
avoidWhen: A true image node or semantic label is more appropriate.

authorMustState: value as literal text, bind form, or format+binds form.

textfield.purpose: Single-line text input bound to state.
preferWhen: The user edits a single-line string value.
avoidWhen: The field is not state-bound.
You are composing a fake field from shape/text/button nodes.
authorMustState: bind key.
Only supported enum values.
event.change when input changes should trigger actions.

slider.purpose: Continuous or stepped numeric range control bound to state.
preferWhen: A numeric value should be adjusted by dragging within a range.
avoidWhen: The value is not range-based.
You are building a manual stepper-like substitute for a range control.
authorMustState: bind, min, and max.
step only when the value should be discrete.
supportNotes: accentColor is the primary runtime-tinted slider property today.
thumbColor, trackColorActive, and trackColorInactive remain part of the declared authoring surface but currently have limited SwiftUI/runtime support.

preferWhen: A boolean preference or on/off choice is being authored.

shape.purpose: Geometric visual node that can optionally host children on top.
preferWhen: The UI needs semantic geometry or a shaped visual backdrop.
avoidWhen: A plain backgroundColor on an existing node is enough.
The node would exist only to simulate a card where group or frame already captures intent better.
supportNotes: cornerStyle remains part of the declared authoring surface for forward compatibility even though the current renderer primarily uses cornerRadius.

frame.purpose: Layout container for explicit frame constraints and alignment of a subtree.
preferWhen: You need explicit alignment or sizing around a subtree.
The node exists to control frame behavior, not to express a higher-level semantic pattern.
avoidWhen: A semantic container already captures the intent and no explicit frame control is needed.

preferWhen: Freeform content must scroll.
A horizontal scroller is needed.
avoidWhen: The content is better modeled as a list of rows.
rendererDoes: Standard scroll content flow does not need an extra wrapper node.
Add an inner layout wrapper only when local spacing, alignment, or grouping decisions require it.

useFor: Freeform vertical arrangement of heterogeneous content.
doNotDefaultTo: Settings screens that should be list-based.
Titled subsections that should be group-based.
Navigation screens that should be navigationstack-based.

useFor: Row internals when no dedicated semantic node exists.
doNotDefaultTo: icon + text labels that should be label.
Hand-built button labels that should be button props.
Cases where shape already gives the desired background + child overlay semantics.

spacer.purpose: Flexible empty space in stack layouts.

notes: Use badge for compact counts or status chips, usually inside a larger semantic row.
